response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and cloud-native architectures, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is a hard requirement and completely absent from his profile. Beyond the language barrier, his experience lacks any depth in embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, Layer-2/3 security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel modification, or chip-vendor SDKs. His C/C++ exposure is minimal and dated. He would require near-complete retraining for the core responsibilities of this role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across multiple languages and domains, demonstrating adaptability
Some embedded and low-latency systems exposure (National Instruments C/C++ work, high-performance async I/O at Sociomantic)
Solid Linux familiarity and open-source infrastructure experience (GitLab, Docker, CI/CD)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement and an immediate disqualifier
Zero experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips, BIOS/bootloader) — the entire core technical domain is absent
No Linux kernel source modification or firmware development experience; career is almost entirely in web/SaaS/cloud application development
